section.name: statistical-methodology
section.finding: Pooling per-volume metrics across folds: within a single CV split, the 70 test-set scores are genuinely independent. Paired bootstrap on per-volume differences (N=70) is valid. However, any parametric test on fold-level aggregates (N=3) MUST use the Nadeau-Bengio correction via correctipy. Grandvalet-Bengio (2004) proved this variance cannot be unbiasedly estimated.

Friedman+Nemenyi is severely underpowered for K=3 folds. Nemenyi CD is too wide; with 3 data points, Wilcoxon minimum p-value is 0.125 (impossible to reach 0.05). Solution: use per-volume paired bootstrap (N=70) as PRIMARY inference. Keep Friedman only as a supplementary omnibus screening test with explicit power-limitation caveat.

Two-tier multiple comparison correction: Holm-Bonferroni for PRIMARY metric (clDice, 6 pairwise tests), BH-FDR for SECONDARY metrics (7 metrics x 6 pairs = 42 tests). Pre-register clDice as primary per CLAUDE.md loss experiment.

Bayesian signed-rank test with ROPE via baycomp: can confirm equivalence (BF < 1/3), not just fail to reject null. Three-way: P(A>B), P(A≈B), P(B>A). ROPE values: DSC ±0.01, clDice ±0.01, HD95 ±0.5 voxel, NSD ±0.01.

Report three effect sizes: Cohen's d (literature comparability), Cliff's delta (non-parametric), Vargha-Delaney A (clinical interpretability = P(A>B)). VDA = (Cliff's delta + 1) / 2. Thresholds: negligible <0.56, small 0.56-0.64, medium 0.64-0.71, large >0.71.

ICC(2,1) is correct for cross-fold consistency (volumes=targets, folds=raters). Bland-Altman is WRONG here — it's for comparing two measurement methods, not cross-validation folds. Drop Bland-Altman from flow unless comparing annotators.
challengeR R package for bootstrap ranking aggregation and stability visualization. Optional — Python-only MVP, R as Phase 2 addition.

section.name: visualization
section.finding: Raincloud plots (ptitprince) replace box plots for N=70 volumes. Shows raw data + distribution + summary simultaneously. Outlier volumes (e.g., mv02 at 4.97μm spacing) visible as individual points.
DABEST estimation plots for pairwise comparisons. Gardner-Altman paired plot shows magnitude and direction of effect with bootstrap CI. Complements p-value tables with visual evidence.
scikit-posthocs (v0.11.2) for CD diagrams + Nemenyi. Single dependency covers both statistical test and visualization. Caveat: CD diagram underpowered for K=3.
Okabe-Ito 8-color palette for colorblind safety. First 4 for 4 loss functions. Yellow (#F0E442) has poor contrast on white — substitute Blue (#0072B2) if needed.
Skip radar charts entirely — effect size heatmap covers same space without perceptual distortions. Add parallel coordinates for multi-metric profiles.

section.name: provenance-reporting
section.finding: 124 studies on 2 Kaggle health datasets scored 0/9 on TRIPOD+AI data provenance. 3 deployed in clinical practice, 1 in a patent — from likely fabricated data. Automated lineage makes this failure mode structurally impossible.

TRIPOD+AI 27-item checklist. Key items for biostatistics flow: Item 10 (sample size), Item 12a (data partitioning), Item 12c (model building), Item 12e (performance measures), Item 23a (CIs for performance).
MI-CLAIM 6 categories: study design, data partitioning, optimization, performance evaluation, model examination, reproducible pipeline. Auto-checkable from MLflow metadata + DuckDB.

item.functions: paired_bootstrap_test, holm_bonferroni_correction, cohens_d, compute_all_pairwise_comparisons, format_comparison_latex, format_significance_matrix_markdown, PairwiseComparison

phase.name: Config and Data Types
phase.description: Pydantic config model, result dataclasses, and source run manifest types. Zero external dependencies — pure Python dataclasses + Pydantic.

task.name: BiostatisticsConfig Pydantic model
task.description: BiostatisticsConfig with fields: experiment_names (list[str]), mlruns_dir (Path), output_dir (Path), metrics (list[str]), primary_metric (str, default="cldice"), alpha (float, 0.05), n_bootstrap (int, 10_000), rope_values (dict[str, float]), min_folds_per_condition (int, 3), min_conditions (int, 2), seed (int, 42). Validates: alpha in (0,1), n_bootstrap > 100, primary_metric in metrics list.

task.name: Result and manifest dataclasses
task.description: SourceRun(run_id, experiment_id, experiment_name, loss_function, fold_id, status). SourceRunManifest(runs, fingerprint=SHA256, discovered_at). ValidationResult(valid, warnings, errors, n_conditions, n_folds_per_condition). PairwiseResult(condition_a, condition_b, metric, p_value, p_adjusted, correction_method, significant, cohens_d, cliffs_delta, vda, bayesian_left, bayesian_rope, bayesian_right). VarianceDecompositionResult(metric, friedman_statistic, friedman_p, nemenyi_matrix, icc_value, icc_ci_lower, icc_ci_upper, icc_type). RankingResult(metric, condition_ranks, mean_ranks, cd_value). FigureArtifact(figure_id, title, paths, sidecar_path). TableArtifact(table_id, title, path, format). BiostatisticsResult(manifest, db_path, pairwise, variance, rankings, figures, tables, mlflow_run_id).

task.name: Default YAML config
task.description: Default config YAML with: experiment_names=[dynunet_loss_variation_v2], metrics=[dsc, hd95, assd, nsd, cldice, be_0, be_1, junction_f1], primary_metric=cldice, alpha=0.05, n_bootstrap=10000, rope_values={dsc: 0.01, cldice: 0.01, hd95: 0.5, nsd: 0.01}. Test that YAML loads into BiostatisticsConfig without error.

phase.name: DuckDB Foundation
phase.description: Multi-experiment DuckDB extraction with per-volume metrics table, source run discovery, and completeness validation.

task.name: Extended DuckDB schema + extraction
task.description: Extend existing duckdb_extraction.py with: experiment_name + experiment_id columns in runs table, new per_volume_metrics table (run_id, fold_id, volume_id, metric_name, metric_value), new ensemble_members table. build_biostatistics_duckdb(manifest, mlruns_dir, output_path) -> Path. Full rebuild every time (small dataset: ~840 metric rows). Export Parquet alongside DuckDB for portability.
task.tests: - test_schema_has_7_tables - test_runs_table_has_experiment_columns - test_per_volume_metrics_table_exists - test_parquet_export_alongside_duckdb - test_extraction_from_mock_mlruns - test_idempotent_rebuild

task.name: Source run discovery
task.description: discover_source_runs(mlruns_dir, experiment_names) -> SourceRunManifest. Reads mlruns filesystem, finds all FINISHED runs in specified experiments. Returns SourceRunManifest with SHA256 fingerprint for idempotency. Reuses mlruns_inspector.py reading functions.

task.name: Source completeness validation
task.description: validate_source_completeness(manifest, min_folds, min_conditions) -> ValidationResult. Checks: all conditions have >= min_folds completed folds, no FAILED/RUNNING, at least min_conditions conditions, per-volume metric arrays exist. Raises BiostatisticsValidationError on critical failures.

phase.name: Statistical Engine
phase.description: Core statistical computations: pairwise tests, effect sizes, Bayesian analysis, variance decomposition. All functions are pure (no Prefect dependency) — they read from DuckDB and return result dataclasses.

task.name: Fix CI averaging bug + add Cliff's delta + VDA
task.description: 1. Fix _summarise_metric() CI averaging bug — average of CI endpoints is NOT a valid CI. Replace with pooled BCa bootstrap. 2. Add cliffs_delta(a, b) -> float. Formula: np.sign(a[:,None] - b[None,:]). Thresholds: |δ| < 0.147 negligible, < 0.33 small, < 0.474 medium, >= 0.474 large. 3. Add vargha_delaney_a(a, b) -> float. VDA = (cliffs_delta + 1) / 2. 4. Add interpret_cliffs_delta(d) -> str and interpret_vda(a) -> str.

task.name: Pairwise comparisons from DuckDB
task.description: compute_pairwise_comparisons(db_path, config) -> list[PairwiseResult]. For each metric x condition pair: 1. Query per-volume metrics from DuckDB 2. BCa bootstrap CI (B=10k, pooled N=70) 3. Wilcoxon signed-rank test (scipy.stats.wilcoxon) 4. Cohen's d, Cliff's delta, VDA (from comparison.py) 5. Two-tier MCC: Holm for primary metric, BH-FDR for secondary Uses existing paired_bootstrap_test() + bca_bootstrap_ci().
task.tests: - test_pairwise_returns_correct_number_of_comparisons - test_primary_metric_uses_holm_correction - test_secondary_metrics_use_bh_fdr - test_all_three_effect_sizes_computed - test_wilcoxon_p_value_present - test_symmetric_pairs (A vs B same as B vs A with sign flip)

task.name: Bayesian signed-rank test with ROPE
task.description: compute_bayesian_comparisons(db_path, config) -> list[PairwiseResult update]. For each condition pair x primary metric: 1. baycomp.SignedRankTest.probs(scores_A, scores_B, rope=config.rope) 2. Returns P(A>B), P(A≈B within ROPE), P(B>A) 3. Updates PairwiseResult.bayesian_left/rope/right fields Optional dependency — gracefully skip if baycomp not installed.

task.name: Variance decomposition (Friedman + ICC)
task.description: compute_variance_decomposition(db_path, config) -> list[VarianceDecompositionResult]. For each metric: 1. Friedman test (scipy.stats.friedmanchisquare) — K=3 blocks x conditions 2. Nemenyi post-hoc (scikit_posthocs.posthoc_nemenyi_friedman) if significant 3. ICC(2,1) via pingouin.intraclass_corr — fold consistency per metric Report power limitations explicitly in results.

task.name: Multi-metric ranking and champion confirmation
task.description: compute_rankings(db_path, pairwise_results, config) -> list[RankingResult]. 1. Per-metric ranking of conditions (lower rank = better) 2. Mean rank across all metrics (Demsar rank-then-aggregate) 3. Critical Difference value for CD diagram 4. Champion confirmation: does statistical evidence support each champion category? Reuses existing rank_then_aggregate() from champion_tagger.py.

phase.name: Figure Generation
phase.description: Publication-quality figures with JSON sidecars. Each figure produces PNG + SVG + JSON sidecar. Matplotlib + seaborn + specialized libs. 11 figure types in 3 tiers (must-have, should-have, nice-to-have).

task.name: Figure generation engine + JSON sidecars
task.description:
generate_figures(db_path, pairwise, variance, rankings, config, output_dir) -> list[FigureArtifact].
Tier 1 (must-have, paper main text): F1: Per-metric condition comparison — raincloud plot (ptitprince) F2: Cross-condition pairwise effect size matrix — heatmap (Cliff's delta) F3: Critical Difference diagram — scikit-posthocs CD diagram F4: Forest plot — per-metric BCa CIs F5: Training curves overlay — line plot with shaded CI
Tier 2 (should-have, supplementary): F6: Fold stability heatmap F7: Metric correlation matrix (Spearman) F8: DABEST estimation plot (paired mean difference)
Color: Okabe-Ito palette (first 4 colors for 4 losses). Export: PNG (300 DPI) + SVG/PDF (vector) + JSON sidecar. matplotlib rcParams: font.size=9, pdf.fonttype=42, dpi=300.
task.tests: - test_figure_generates_png_and_svg - test_json_sidecar_has_required_fields - test_sidecar_data_field_has_plotting_arrays - test_sidecar_statistical_tests_field - test_sidecar_source_lineage_fields - test_colorblind_palette_used - test_figure_count_matches_config

task.name: JSON sidecar schema and writer
task.description: FigureSidecar dataclass with fields: figure_id, figure_title, generated_at, git_commit, software_versions, source (duckdb_artifact, query, source_run_ids, experiment_name), data_summary (n_conditions, n_volumes, conditions), data (per-condition: values, mean, ci_lower, ci_upper, n), statistical_tests (pairwise list, friedman dict). write_sidecar(sidecar, path) -> Path. Self-contained: re-plottable without DuckDB access.

task.name: LaTeX table generation
task.description:
generate_tables(pairwise, variance, rankings, config, output_dir) -> list[TableArtifact].
Tables: T1: Main comparison table — metrics x conditions with CIs + significance markers T2: Pairwise p-value matrix — per metric, Holm-Bonferroni corrected T3: Effect size table — Cohen's d + Cliff's delta + VDA T4: Variance decomposition — Friedman statistic, ICC T5: Champion summary — 3 categories with statistical evidence
Uses booktabs (toprule/midrule/bottomrule), textbf for best values. Reuses format_comparison_latex() pattern from comparison.py.
task.tests: - test_comparison_table_has_booktabs - test_best_values_are_bold - test_significance_markers_present - test_champion_summary_has_3_categories - test_tables_compile_without_latex_errors (syntax check only)

task.name: Lineage manifest generation
task.description: build_lineage_manifest(manifest, config, figures, tables) -> dict. Generates lineage.json with schema_version, biostatistics_run_id, generated_at, fingerprint, git_commit, git_branch, git_dirty, environment (python, torch, scipy, duckdb versions), source_experiments (name, id, run_ids, conditions per run), dataset_provenance (name, version, n_volumes, source, ethics), tripod_ai_items (9 items with automated evidence), artifacts_produced (duckdb, figures, tables, parquet), statistical_methods (primary_ci, pairwise_test, effect_sizes, seed).

task.name: MLflow run logging
task.description: log_biostatistics_run(manifest, db_path, figures, tables, pairwise, variance, lineage) -> str (run_id). Creates ONE mlrun in 'minivess_biostatistics' experiment. Logs artifacts: biostatistics.duckdb, parquet/, figures/ (PNG+SVG), figures/sidecars/ (JSON), tables/ (.tex), lineage.json. Tags: upstream_fingerprint, upstream_experiment_names, upstream_lineage_artifact, n_source_runs, n_conditions, n_metrics, biostat_generated_at, tripod_ai_coverage.

task.name: Prefect flow assembly
Trigger chain + deployment integration
task.description: 1. Add "biostatistics" to _DEFAULT_FLOWS in trigger.py after "analyze" (position 5, best-effort). Update flow count 8->9. 2. Add to deployments.py: "biostatistics": "cpu-pool" (FLOW_WORK_POOL_MAP), "biostatistics": "minivess-biostatistics:latest" (FLOW_IMAGE_MAP). 3. Update existing tests: flow count, flow order, chain integration.

task.name: Integration test
task.description: Full pipeline test: mock mlruns -> DuckDB -> statistics -> figures -> tables -> MLflow run. Creates synthetic mlruns structure with 4 conditions x 3 folds, runs the full flow, validates all output artifacts exist and are well-formed.

task.name: Dockerfile
task.description: CPU-only Dockerfile for biostatistics flow. FROM minivess-base:latest. No GPU needed — pure statistical computation. Installs: duckdb, scikit-posthocs, pingouin, baycomp, matplotlib.

verification.command: uv run ruff check src/ tests/
uv run pytest tests/ -x -q && uv run ruff check src/ tests/ && uv run mypy src/

execution-protocol.rule: Each task follows TDD: RED (write failing test) → GREEN (implement minimum) → VERIFY (test+lint+type) → FIX → CHECKPOINT (git commit) → CONVERGE
Commit after each task passes. Commit message: "feat(biostatistics): Task X.Y — description"
If a task fails, do NOT move to next task. Fix the current task first.
Run full test suite after each phase completes to catch regressions.
Before implementing any task, re-read the task description AND the relevant section of docs/planning/biostatistics-prefect-flow.md.
All statistical functions have ZERO Prefect dependency. Only the flow assembly (Phase 8) imports @task/@flow decorators.
Never hardcode metric names, loss names, or experiment names in statistical functions. Everything config-driven.
New dependencies: run "uv add {package}" BEFORE writing import statements. Verify import works.
If context is lost mid-session: read this XML plan, check git log for last completed task, resume from next task.

level.name: per-volume-paired
level.step: Effect sizes: Cohen's d, Cliff's delta, Vargha-Delaney A

level.name: multiple-comparison-correction
level.step: Secondary metrics (7 x 6 = 42 tests): Benjamini-Hochberg FDR

level.name: fold-level
level.step: Friedman test (K=3 blocks) — report power limitation

level.name: reporting
level.step: Per-metric: mean, median, IQR, 95% BCa CI
Exact metric implementations with version pins (MONAI)

tier.name: must-have
tier.figure: Cross-condition pairwise effect size matrix (Cliff's delta)
Critical Difference diagram (8 metrics, K=3 caveat)
Training curves overlay with shaded CI

tier.name: should-have
tier.figure: DABEST Cumming estimation plot (paired mean difference)

tier.name: nice-to-have
tier.figure: Bayesian three-way probability bars (P(A>B), P(rope), P(B>A))

references.ref: Nadeau C, Bengio Y. (2003). Inference for the Generalization Error. Machine Learning.
Grandvalet Y, Bengio Y. (2004). No Unbiased Estimator of the Variance of K-Fold CV. JMLR 5.
Demšar J. (2006). Statistical Comparisons of Classifiers over Multiple Data Sets. JMLR 7.
Garcia S, Herrera F. (2008). Extension on Statistical Comparisons. JMLR 9.
Benavoli A, Corani G, Demšar J, Zaffalon M. (2017). Time for a Change. JMLR 18.
Maier-Hein L, et al. (2024). Metrics Reloaded. Nature Methods 21(2).
Reinke A, et al. (2024). Understanding Metric-Related Pitfalls. Nature Methods.
Wiesenfarth M, et al. (2021). challengeR. Scientific Reports 11.
Collins GS, et al. (2024). TRIPOD+AI Statement. BMJ.
Gibson AD, et al. (2026). Evidence of Unreliable Data Provenance. medRxiv.
Norgeot B, et al. (2020). MI-CLAIM. Nature Medicine.
Allen M, et al. (2019). Raincloud Plots. Wellcome Open Research 4:63.
Ho J, et al. (2019). Moving Beyond P Values: DABEST. Nature Methods.
Herbold S. (2020). Autorank. JOSS.
Vargha A, Delaney HD. (2000). Common Language Effect Size. JEBS 25(2).
Koo TK, Li MY. (2016). ICC Guideline. J Chiropractic Medicine.
Romano J, et al. (2006). Appropriate Statistics for Ordinal Level Data. JGME.
Efron B. (1987). Better Bootstrap Confidence Intervals. JASA 82(397).
Abrahamsson N, et al. (2017). Multiple Comparisons. BMC Med Inform Decis Mak.
Van Calster B, et al. (2024). Performance Measure Taxonomy. STRATOS.
Riley RD, Collins GS. (2023). Multiverse of Clinical Prediction Models.
